title: Nephrodium obtusatum
taxonomy: Nephrodium obtusatum is classified as a type of extinct fern, specifically belonging to the family Matonidaceae. It is a member of the class Polypodiopsida and is also known as a member of the order Matoniales. This extinct fern is closely related to the modern fern genus Matonia. Further classification includes Nephrodium obtusatum being a genus of extinct ferns within the phylum Pteridophyta. The species is characterized by its unique leaf morphology and is considered a distinct fossil record of ancient plant evolution.
uses: Nephrodium obtusatum, also known as the obtuse lady fern, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's fronds, in particular, have been utilized to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. Its medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. In some cultures, the plant is also believed to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal uses.

parts: Nephrodium obtusatum is actually a synonym for the fern species Blechnum spicant. Nephrodium obtusatum is not widely reported for medicinal use in traditional practices. intro: Nephrodium obtusatum, also known as Maidenhair Fern, is a species of fern belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Nephrodium obtusatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Nephrodium obtusatum is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, as well as being used in poultices and infusions to treat various ailments.
